evals.prompt: Run a live A2A end-to-end check through the Omni Agent desktop UI. Retrieve the enabled server's agent card first, choose a safe read-only skill, ask a realistic user question, and show me proof that the UI delegated to that A2A tool.
evals.expected_output: Uses the project skill, performs card-first discovery, refuses browser mocks, selects only read-only skills, requires tool-call/tool-result/done/visible-answer evidence, and reports redacted artifacts plus cleanup.
assertions: Agent card retrieval is required before any UI prompt
The real Tauri UI is driven rather than browser mock mode
Only clearly read-only A2A skills are eligible
PASS requires matching A2A tool-call and tool-result events plus a visible answer
Artifacts are redacted and child processes are cleaned up

evals.prompt: Verify only the inventory connection's list-assets skill on the live A2A server, using the actual desktop composer. Do not run anything that changes server state.
evals.expected_output: Uses connection and skill filters without overriding the read-only safety classifier, drives the native UI, and reports exact delegation evidence or a bounded BLOCKED/FAIL diagnosis.
assertions: Connection and skill filters are passed to the runner
The selected card-derived A2A approval is handled once through the UI; other approval prompts fail
A plausible answer without delegation evidence is not accepted

evals.prompt: Diagnose whether this checkout is ready for live A2A UI E2E without invoking any live skill yet. I want agent-card discovery and actionable setup gaps only.
evals.expected_output: Runs the dry-run path, retrieves cards first, derives/skips questions safely, does not launch the UI or invoke a skill, and reports precise missing prerequisites with redacted artifacts.
assertions: Uses --dry-run and does not launch the UI
Retrieves enabled connection cards with canonical and legacy fallback paths
Never prints saved tokens or provider credentials
Reports actionable prerequisite or discovery failures
